---
title: "A549-HBE-ALI comparison analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=F}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

source("modules/helpers.R")
library(ggVennDiagram)
library(patchwork)
library(ComplexUpset)

library(tidyverse)
```

<!-- All set up!!! Repo is at https://github.com/axg610/a549-hbe-ali-comparison. -->

```{r load data}
dea <- read_tsv("data/raw/A549vsPrimary_univariateDEA.txt")
tpm <- read_tsv("data/raw/A549vsPrimary_tpm.txt")
multivar <- read_tsv("data/raw/A549vsPrimary_multivariateDEA.txt")
```

## Alignment and DEA

**Raw datasets:** A549, primary hBECs (submersion culture, HBE), and primary hBECs (air-liquid interface culture, ALI) treated with IL1B, glucocorticoid, or both at 6h. This is actually three experiments at different timepoints, so for now we use their shared timepoint (6 h post-treatment).

Alignment performed with kallisto version 0.46.1 and normalization/DEA performed with sleuth version something on the cluster. Everything documented in `kallisto-sleuth.md`. I used individual sleuth runs for each cell type, modeled as `~treatment`. Effect sizes ("log2fold") and FDR are from Wald test using unstimulated (NS) samples as baseline. I also modeled everything together under model `~treatment*condition` for interaction testing. Finally, raw tpm values were extracted.

```{r data previews}
knitr::kable(
  dea %>% filter(Gene == "NFKBIA"),
  caption = "Example univariate DEA data (~treatment)", 
  align = "c"
)

knitr::kable(
  multivar %>% filter(Gene %in% c("NFKBIA", "TNFAIP3", "IRAK3")),
  caption = "Example multivariate DEA data (~treatment)", 
  align = "c"
)

knitr::kable(
  tpm %>% filter(Gene == "NFKBIA") %>% .[1:20,],
  caption = "Example tpm data, first 20 rows for representative gene", 
  align = "c"
)
```

## IN PROGRESS: Understanding individual variation in gene expression

Previously, we saw that the IL1B-regulated gene signature in A549 cells is maybe only ~30% shared with the primary cell types. Venn diagram for "significant" genes (log2fold <= -1 or >= 1 & FDR < 0.05) in each cell type:

```{r old venn, out.width='50%'}
knitr::include_graphics("data/il1b-venn.png")
```

One possible reason: A549s are derived from one individual, so four sequencing replicates may have low variability + higher significance. Each sequencing replicate for primary cells is a different individual, so higher variability + lower significance. Ignoring FDR entirely (i.e., only using the abs(log2fold) >= 1 threshold), A549 and primary cells actually have similarly sized regulated gene pools (Fig A). In A549s, variation in tpm-derived log2fold values tend to be lower (Fig B) and a greater proportion of the rep pool meets log2fold thresholds (Fig C).

*Fixed problem 040626: discrepancy between Venn and spread gene counts.*

```{r initialize per-donor data}
per_donor_data <- list()

per_donor_data[["tpm_by_donor"]] <- tpm %>%
  filter(treatment == "IL1B") %>%
  mutate(rep = if_else(celltype == "A549", "mean", rep)) %>%
  mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
  select(Gene, name, rep, celltype, treatment, tpm, log2tpm, fold, log2fold)
  
per_donor_data[["thresholded_log2fold"]] <- tpm_by_donor %>%
  group_by(Gene, name, rep, celltype) %>%
  summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(meets_threshold = abs(log2fold) >= 1)

cleanup_objects()
```


```{r log2fold threshold on a per-donor basis, fig.height=3, fig.width=8}

# counts: how many threshold-meeting genes in each donor?
counts <- per_donor_data[["thresholded_log2fold"]] %>%
  filter(meets_threshold) %>%
  group_by(name, rep, celltype) %>%
  summarize(n = length(unique(Gene)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(name) %>%
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = unique(name)))

# spread: sd and %meetingThreshold for each gene, in each celltype
spread <- per_donor_data[["thresholded_log2fold"]] %>%
  group_by(Gene, celltype) %>%
  filter(any(meets_threshold)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-log2fold) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  left_join(
    per_donor_data[["tpm_by_donor"]]
  ) %>%
  group_by(Gene, celltype) %>%
  summarize(
    sd = sd(log2fold),
    n_meets_threshold = length(Gene[abs(log2fold) >= 1]),
    nreps = length(Gene),
    percent_meets_threshold = 100 * n_meets_threshold / nreps,
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# plots

p1 <- ggplot(
  counts, 
  aes(y = name, x = n, fill = celltype, label = n)
) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(hjust = 1.1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "genes with |log2fold| > 1", y = NULL, title = "A") +
  guides(fill = "none")

p2 <- ggplot(
  spread,
  aes(y = celltype, x = sd, fill = celltype)
) +
  geom_jitter(size = 0.1, height = 0.3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, outliers = F, alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "SD of log2fold across donors", title = "B") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 3)) +
  guides(fill = "none")

p3 <- ggplot(
  spread,
  aes(y = celltype, x = percent_meets_threshold, fill = celltype)
) +
  stat_summary(
    geom = "errorbar", fun.data = "mean_sdl",
    fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
    width = 0.5
    ) +
  stat_summary(
    geom = "col", fun = "mean",
    color = "black"
    ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "% donors with |log2fold| > 1", title = "C")

wrap_plots(p1, p2, p3, nrow = 1)

cleanup_objects()
```

Something Rob asked for: for each primary cell donor, check overlap of genes with abs(log2fold) > 1 in that primary cell **(white)** vs A549 cells **(grey)**. I also tagged the plots with Jaccard index **(J)** and total number of genes **(n)**

```{r pairwise venns fig.width=7}

# sets: which genes meet the log2fold threshold in each donor?
sets <- per_donor_data[["thresholded_log2fold"]] %>%
  filter(meets_threshold) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(genes = list(unique(Gene)), .groups = "drop")

# a549 genes: the set against which all other sets will be compared
a549_genes <- sets %>%
  filter(name == "A549 mean") %>%
  pull(genes) %>%
  .[[1]]

# overlaps: how does each donor interact with the A549 consensus?
overlaps <- sets %>%
  mutate(
    n_genes = map_int(genes, length),
    overlap_genes = map(genes, ~ intersect(a549_genes, .x)),
    overlap = map_int(genes, ~ length(intersect(a549_genes, .x))),
    union = map_int(genes, ~ length(union(a549_genes, .x))),
    jaccard = overlap / union,
    title = paste0(
      name,
      "\nJ=", round(jaccard, 2),
      " | n=", union
    )
  )

# plots

plots <- overlaps %>%
  mutate(
    p = map2(
      genes,
      title,
      ~ ggVennDiagram(
        list(
          " " = a549_genes,
          "  " = .x
        ),
        label_alpha = 0,
        label_size = 3
      ) +
        ggtitle(.y) +
        scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "grey50") +
        theme(
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5)
        )
    )
  )

wrap_plots(plots$p, ncol = 6)

cleanup_objects()
```










Visualize set relations: where does the IL1B-"regulated" transcriptional profile of each donor sit in relation to all other donors? In relation to the A549 mean?

**TODO: clean up UpSet plot code. What do we actually want to intersect?**

```{r upset plot}

# TODO: clean this up

gene_sets_upset <- tpm %>%
  mutate(rep = if_else(str_detect(rep, "R"), "mean", rep)) %>%
  filter(
    treatment == "IL1B",
    abs(log2fold) >= 1,
    celltype %in% c("A549", "ALI")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Gene, rep, celltype) %>%
  summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(genes = list(unique(Gene)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  unnest(genes) %>%
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = name,
    values_from = value,
    values_fill = 0
  )

upset(
  gene_sets_upset,
  intersect = colnames(gene_sets_upset)[-1],
  name = "Shared genes",
  width_ratio = 0.2,
  min_size = 50
)

gene_sets_upset <- tpm %>%
  mutate(rep = if_else(str_detect(rep, "R"), "mean", rep)) %>%
  filter(
    treatment == "IL1B",
    abs(log2fold) >= 1,
    celltype %in% c("A549", "HBE")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Gene, rep, celltype) %>%
  summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(genes = list(unique(Gene)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  unnest(genes) %>%
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = name,
    values_from = value,
    values_fill = 0
  )

upset(
  gene_sets_upset,
  intersect = colnames(gene_sets_upset)[-1],
  name = "Shared genes",
  width_ratio = 0.2,
  min_size = 50
)

gene_sets_upset <- tpm %>%
  mutate(rep = if_else(str_detect(rep, "R"), "mean", rep)) %>%
  filter(
    treatment == "IL1B",
    abs(log2fold) >= 1,
    celltype %in% c("ALI", "HBE")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Gene, rep, celltype) %>%
  summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(genes = list(unique(Gene)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  unnest(genes) %>%
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = name,
    values_from = value,
    values_fill = 0
  )

upset(
  gene_sets_upset,
  intersect = colnames(gene_sets_upset)[-1],
  name = "Shared genes",
  width_ratio = 0.2,
  min_size = 50
)

cleanup_objects()
```

Rob: *"Something I don’t understand: The number of A549 DEGs has jumped from about 2600 in the first analysis (Venn) to 8200 here (above analysis)?? Something wrong? Is the artificial baseline (tpm 0.1 or 0.01) at play here? The simple tpm calculation will then include many very lowly expressed genes…..?"*

Baseline is 0.1, code snippet below:

```{text, echo=T, eval=F}

  ...
    filter(!all(tpm == 0)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(log2tpm = log2(tpm + 0.1)) %>%
    group_by(Gene, celltype) %>%
    mutate(log2fold = log2tpm - log2tpm[treatment == "NS"]) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(fold = 2^log2fold) %>%
  ...
```


```{r discrepancy in gene number}
gene_to_fdr <- dea %>%
  filter(treatment == "IL1B") %>%
  select(Gene, celltype, FDR)

# df <- tpm %>%
#   mutate(rep = if_else(str_detect(rep, "R"), "mean", rep)) %>%
#   filter(treatment == "IL1B") %>%
#   mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
#   group_by(Gene, rep, celltype, name) %>%
#   summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
#   ungroup() %>%
#   filter(
#     abs(log2fold) >= 1
#   ) %>%
#   left_join(gene_to_fdr, by = c("Gene", "celltype")) %>%
#   mutate(FDR = if_else(is.na(FDR), 1, FDR)) %>%
#   mutate(sig = FDR <= 0.05)

df <- tpm %>%
  mutate(rep = if_else(str_detect(rep, "R"), "mean", rep)) %>%
  filter(treatment == "IL1B", abs(log2fold) >= 1) %>%
  mutate(name = paste(celltype, rep)) %>%
  group_by(Gene, rep, celltype, name) %>%
  summarize(log2fold = mean(log2fold), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(gene_to_fdr, by = c("Gene", "celltype")) %>%
  mutate(FDR = if_else(is.na(FDR), 1, FDR)) %>%
  mutate(sig = FDR <= 0.05)

ggplot(
  df,
  aes(x = log2fold, fill = sig)
) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free_x") +
  geom_histogram()
```
